items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.
Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.correct: Two or more independent melodic lines sounding together.
items.misconception: A single melody with chord accompaniment.
items.why: Students confuse polyphony with thick texture or homophony. Polyphony specifically means INDEPENDENT MELODIES - like a fugue. Melody + chords is HOMOPHONY, not polyphony.
Correct answer: A cappella. “Continuo” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Same music for each verse is:
items.why: It's Strophic. “Through-composed” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If a strophic song has 4 verses, how many times is the same melody sung?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
